title: Our favorite gear at Sea Otter Classic wasn't the bikes—it was the accessories
description: A Bluetooth suction-cup rack and a palm-sized tow rope were among our most practical finds.

title: Investors lost billions on Trump’s memecoin. Another gala won’t fix that.
description: If Dems take Congress, Trump may face reckoning for “pay-to-play” memecoin galas.

title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
description: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. By the end of 2025, half of companies used AI in at least t

title: 3 things Michelle Kim is into right now
description: Isegye Idol If you thought K-pop was weird, virtual idols—humans who perform as anime-style digital characters via motion capture—will blow your mind. My favorite is a girl group called Isegye Idol, created by Woowakgood, a Korean VTuber (a streamer who likewise performs as a digital persona). Isegy

title: One town’s scheme to get rid of its geese
description: “Pull over!” I order my brother one sunny February afternoon. Our target is in sight: a gaggle of Canada geese, pecking at grass near the dog park. As I approach, tiptoeing over their grayish-white poop, I notice that one bird wears a white cuff around its slender black neck. It’s a GPS tracker—part

title: There is no nature anymore
description: When people talk about “nature,” they’re generally talking about things that aren’t made by human beings. Rocks. Reefs. Red wolves. But while there is plenty of God’s creation to go around, it is hard to think of anything on Earth that human hands haven’t affected. In the Brazilian rainforest, scien

title: Los Angeles is finally going underground
description: Los Angeles deserves its reputation as the quintessential car city—the rhythms of its 2,200 square miles are dictated by wide boulevards and concrete arcs of freeways. But it once had a world-class rail transit system, and for the last three decades, the city has been rebuilding a network of trolley

title: Join Our Livestream: Musk v. Altman and the Future of OpenAI
description: On May 8, we’re going live to answer your questions about the Musk v. Altman trial that could determine the fate of OpenAI.

title: The Pope’s Warnings About AI Were AI-Generated, a Detection Tool Claims
description: Pangram Labs’ updated Chrome extension puts warning labels on AI slop as you scroll your social feeds.

title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
description: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
description: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
description: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the same companies now competing directly with Cursor for the developer market.

title: Pentagon wants $54B for drones, more than most nations’ military budgets
description: The proposed Pentagon drone investment rivals Ukraine’s entire military budget.

title: Mozilla: Anthropic's Mythos found 271 security vulnerabilities in Firefox 150
description: CTO says new AI model is "every bit as capable" as world's best security researchers.

title: Supreme Court arguments make it clear that FCC fines are "nonbinding"
description: FCC tells Supreme Court its fines are nonbinding unless a jury upholds penalty.

title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
description: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
description: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

title: OpenAI Beefs Up ChatGPT’s Image Generation Model
description: The ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is here. Our testing shows it’s better at creating more detailed images and rendering text, but it still struggles with languages other than English.

title: Mozilla Used Anthropic’s Mythos to Find and Fix 271 Bugs in Firefox
description: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.

title: Tim Cook’s Legacy Is Turning Apple Into a Subscription
description: The soon-to-exit Apple CEO went all in on services. Now, the incoming CEO, John Ternus, will need to embrace the AI era.

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
